stages.name: unit_tests
stages.evidence: test/runner_baseline_test.zig 'a non-zero command carrying a Zig compile diagnostic classifies as compile_error' drives runner.classifyCommand with a `<path>:<line>:<col>: error: ...` stderr and no test-runner summary and asserts failure_kind == .compile_error; it failed before the fix (found .test_failure). 'a post-compile assertion failure stays test_failure' uses output containing the `0 passed; 0 skipped; 1 failed.` summary and stays .test_failure; 'a bare non-zero exit with no compile diagnostic stays test_failure' keeps the conservative default. test/mutant_runner_test.zig 'mutant whose project fails to compile is classified compile_error, not killed' asserts the terminal ResultStatus is .compile_error (failed before the fix: found .killed). Full suite green (510 tests).

stages.name: real_binary_behavior
stages.command: zig build && ./zig-out/bin/zentinel --config zentinel.toml run --report json  (errdefer_remove over a fixture whose mutant orphans an unused local)
stages.evidence: An errdefer_remove mutant that removes the only use of a local (`const guard: u8 = 7; errdefer ...guard...` -> `errdefer {}`) makes the project fail to compile (`risky.zig:3:11: error: unused local constant`). Before the fix the real binary reported this as status=killed, failure_kind=test_failure (summary killed=1 compile_error=0) -- a compiler rejection falsely credited to the tests. After the fix it reports status=compile_error, failure_kind=compile_error (summary killed=0 compile_error=1), so the headline kill count counts only mutants the tests actually caught.

stages.name: mutation_checks
stages.reason: Task 118 corrects a deterministic-core classification field; the behavioral fix is proven by a failing-before/passing-after classifier and terminal-status unit test plus a real-binary before/after verdict. Full self-mutation dogfood remains gated to task 085.

residual_risk: Detection is a deterministic heuristic for pinned Zig 0.16: a non-zero command is `compile_error` iff its captured output carries a `: error: ` Zig compile diagnostic and lacks the `passed;` test-runner completion summary. The signal is taken only from captured command output, so AI cannot influence failure_kind or status (I-001).

Conservative default: a non-zero command with neither a compile diagnostic nor a test-runner summary (for example a custom non-Zig command that just exits non-zero) stays `test_failure`, preserving the prior classification rather than over-claiming `compile_error`.

Compile failures that emit only a leading `error: ...` line without a `<path>:<line>:<col>:` prefix (rare module/linker errors) are not matched and stay `test_failure`; the F-2 case (normal Zig compile diagnostics for a mutated source file) is matched. classifyCommand is shared with the baseline, where failure_kind is evidence only -- the baseline still fails on any non-passing command, so baseline behavior is unchanged.

terminalStatus already mapped failure_kind == .compile_error to ResultStatus .compile_error; src/mutant_runner.zig needed no change. The report summary already has a compile_error counter (no schema change).
